reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 75 cfs @ Fort Collins , 2 c fs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir Summer is in full swing on the Poudre, which means the bugs have been active and the fish have been too. If you're planning to fish the lower canyon, be mindful of water temperatures. Afternoon temperatures have been exceeding 68°F, which can significantly increase stress and mortality in trout after they're caught and released. If you do fish the lower canyon, your best window is from sunrise until around 11:00 a.m. As for bugs, the menu is wide open on the Poudre. Caddis, PMDs, and Yellow Sallies have been the most active, while Golden Stones and Green Drakes have also been making appearances when conditions are right. Although there's been plenty of bug activity, it's important to remember that the river will continue warming as summer progresses. Fishing earlier in the day and focusing on higher sections of the canyon are great ways to avoid the warmest water temperatures and help protect the fishery. This is also an excellent time of year to explore the higher-elevation creeks and lakes throughout the Poudre drainage, so get out while the fishing is at its best. Dry flies have been producing well, with consistent hatches during the early morning and again in the evening. As bug activity slows through the middle of the day, dry-dropper rigs have been especially effective, with Caddis and PMD patterns leading the way. If you have any questions or are interested in booking a guided trip on the Poudre, feel free to give either shop a call. We'll be happy to help you get set up for a great day on the water. Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year. Or, click the images below for a full list of our current recommended fly patterns.
